name: Lena
note: SYNTHETIC — draft for clinical advisor review. De-identified/fictional. Distinct from Maya exemplar (broad evaluative performance rather than math-specific).

entries.child_behavior_observed: Lena said she felt sick and might fail and everyone would think she's stupid. She studied until midnight and then cried.
entries.parent_response: I told her she's smart and let her stay up to study more.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Lena begged to drop out the week before, said her hands would shake and she'd mess up in front of everyone.
entries.parent_response: I told her teacher she might not perform.

entries.situation: Soccer tryouts for the travel team
entries.child_behavior_observed: Lena pretended to have a hurt ankle so she wouldn't have to try out.
entries.parent_response: I told the coach she was injured.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Lena asked to be moved to an easier group so she wouldn't be the worst one.
entries.parent_response: I emailed the teacher to ask.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Lena said she had a stomach ache and stayed home the day of the bee.

entries.situation: Got a B on a quiz
entries.child_behavior_observed: Lena was inconsolable, said she's a failure, and ripped up the quiz.
entries.parent_response: I told her one B doesn't matter and offered to email the teacher for extra credit.

situations: Evaluative performance (tests, recitals, tryouts, graded reading group, spelling bee)
parental_responses: Accommodating (emailing for easier placement / extra credit)
safety_behaviors: Avoidance/withdrawal (faking injury or illness, staying home, dropping out)
expected_elements.notes: DRAFT answer key — advisor to confirm/correct.
